question: What is the net electrical output considering the self-consumption of the 12 NPM facilities?

nuscale_ch01_0006.title: Power Output
nuscale_ch01_0006.summary: The NuScale Power Plant consists of up to 12 NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), each rated at 160 MWt and producing approximately 50 MWe, for a total net output of about 570 MWe when accounting for house loads. The electrical output may vary depending on environmental conditions, and the design includes a 2% margin for measurement uncertainty.

nuscale_ch01_0008.title: Format and Content
nuscale_ch01_0008.summary: The section describes the NuScale containment vessel as a cylindrical, ASME-certified steel structure designed to withstand high-pressure events, with internal vacuum conditions during normal operation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) that uses natural circulation and eliminates the need for external piping and coolant pumps. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) produces 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe, and applicants referencing this design must provide construction schedules.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses smaller, simpler modules with fewer external systems and enhanced safety features compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions, eliminating the need for insulation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, small modular pressurized water reactor with an integral design that removes the need for external piping and pumps, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant generating a net output of approximately 570 MWe.

edges.description: Section B describes the Central Utility Building's support systems (such as chiller equipment and instrument air) that consume electrical power, which must be subtracted from the gross power output described in Section A to determine the net electrical output.

Section A provides nominal design values for the NuScale Power Plant, which are necessary to determine the gross electrical output, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building and its utility systems that contribute to the plant's self-consumption, both of which are needed to calculate the net electrical output as asked in the analysis question.
